title: Family reunification paper plan
summary: A family reconnect plan for school, work, transport, phone failure, and backup adults.
content: Families split across school, work, shops, and relatives need a plan that does not assume instant calling. The paper plan names collection permissions, backup adults, meeting points, out-of-area contact, medical notes, and safe phrases for children.
steps: Write usual locations by time of day.
Choose a local and out-of-area contact.
Teach children what to say and what stays private.
Keep copies in bags and with trusted caregivers.
warnings: Do not put sensitive details on copies that may be lost.
Do not rely on social media during crisis.
Do not let unverified adults collect children.
common_mistakes: Only one parent knows the plan.
The meeting point is too vague.
The plan forgets people without smartphones.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Family reunification paper plan with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Family reunification paper plan if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Family reunification paper plan and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Family reunification paper plan or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Family reunification paper plan are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Family reunification paper plan”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Family reunification paper plan as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Family reunification paper plan when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Family reunification paper plan.
short_term: For the first hours of Family reunification paper plan, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Family reunification paper plan remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Family reunification paper plan fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Family reunification paper plan to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
